title: Colorblind-safe dark-factory palette
description:
## Summary

Replace the default green snake / red food / black background color triplet with an OKLab-chosen palette: blue-cyan snake (#00CFCF), amber food (#F5A623), near-black background (#0D0D12), and grid lines in dark slate. Verify contrast ratios meet WCAG AA (4.5:1) for any overlaid text. Extract all colors to CSS custom properties at the top of the stylesheet.

## Code references



## Detail

Benefits the deuteranope player who cannot reliably distinguish a green snake from red food against a dark background — a combination that is near-invisible under red-green color blindness affecting ~8% of male players. Cheapest alternative: swap snake to white and food to yellow — a 2-line CSS change that solves 90% of the problem at zero design cost. This proposal adds an OKLab-verified palette with CSS custom properties for future theming; it does NOT buy a runtime theme switcher, high-contrast mode toggle, protanopia-specific tuning, or APCA compliance.

## Out of scope

_none_

## Blockers (external / decisions needed)

_none_

---
*Imported from Arden proposal `snake-007`.*

title: Reduced-motion mode via prefers-reduced-motion
description:
## Summary

Check window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)') on load and expose a runtime toggle button. In reduced-motion mode: double the tick interval (half speed), suppress any flicker/flash effects on death, and remove any canvas shimmer or glow animations. CSS transitions and animations on HUD elements are also suppressed via the media query in the stylesheet.

## Code references



## Detail

Benefits the player with a vestibular disorder or photosensitive epilepsy who experiences motion sickness or seizure risk from fast-moving canvas animations. Cheapest alternative: add the CSS media query alone to suppress CSS transitions — zero JS changes, but the canvas game loop itself runs at full speed, which is the primary motion source. This proposal addresses both CSS and JS motion sources; it does NOT buy a fully static turn-based mode, screen-reader position announcements, or WCAG 2.3 no-flashing compliance certification.

## Out of scope

_none_

## Blockers (external / decisions needed)

_none_

---
*Imported from Arden proposal `snake-008`.*

title: Keyboard focus management and canvas ARIA label
description:
## Summary

Add tabindex='0' and a descriptive aria-label to the canvas element so keyboard-only users can tab to and activate the game. Apply a visible :focus-visible outline styled to the dark-factory theme. On page load, optionally auto-focus the canvas. Add a visually-hidden 'Skip to game' skip-link at the top of the page.

## Code references



## Detail

Benefits the keyboard-only user who tabs to the page and finds the canvas unreachable — the game is currently invisible to non-pointer navigation. Cheapest alternative: add tabindex='0' alone (2-minute change) — gains focus reachability but omits the visible ring and aria-label, leaving screen-reader users with no context. This proposal buys basic focus discoverability; it does NOT buy live-region announcements of score changes, game-state narration, or voice-control support.

## Out of scope

_none_

## Blockers (external / decisions needed)

_none_

---
*Imported from Arden proposal `snake-006`.*

title: Touch swipe controls for mobile play
description:
## Summary

Attach touchstart/touchend listeners to the canvas element. Compute swipe direction from delta-X / delta-Y and feed it to the same direction-update function used by keyboard events. Ignore swipes shorter than a configurable threshold (e.g., 20px) to suppress accidental taps. Add touch-action: none to the canvas CSS to prevent scroll hijacking.

## Code references



## Detail

Benefits the mobile player trying to demo the game on their phone — currently the game is keyboard-only and completely unplayable on touch screens. Cheapest alternative: render four on-screen arrow buttons — simpler touch target logic, but buttons occlude roughly 30% of the canvas on a 375px viewport. Swipe is cleaner for a full-canvas game. This proposal does NOT buy pinch-to-zoom prevention via meta viewport, landscape orientation lock, haptic feedback, or a virtual gamepad.

## Out of scope

_none_

## Blockers (external / decisions needed)

_none_

---
*Imported from Arden proposal `snake-005`.*

title: Wrap-around mode toggle (walls kill vs. tunnel through)
description:
## Summary

Add a checkbox or keyboard shortcut (W) to toggle wrap-around mode before or between games. In wrap mode, a snake leaving the right edge reappears on the left (and top/bottom equivalently). The current mode is shown in the HUD. Toggle is disabled mid-game to prevent exploit-switching.

## Code references



## Detail

Benefits the player who finds instant wall-death frustrating and wants a lower-stakes 'chill' practice mode to improve without constant restarts. Cheapest alternative: permanently remove wall death — one conditional delete, zero UI, but removes the skill ceiling and alienates players who enjoy the wall-death tension. This proposal buys a selectable difficulty axis; it does NOT buy toroidal minimap rendering, diagonal wrapping, or any AI path planning that accounts for the wrap geometry.

## Out of scope

_none_

## Blockers (external / decisions needed)

_none_

---
*Imported from Arden proposal `snake-004`.*

title: Progressive speed curve across 10 levels
description:
## Summary

Introduce a level system: every N apples eaten advances the level (1–10). Each level reduces the game-loop tick interval by 15% from a configurable base speed. The current level and target apple count display as HUD text on the canvas. Constants are extracted to a CONFIG object at the top of the file.

## Code references



## Detail

Benefits the experienced player who finds the default constant speed trivially slow after the first 30 seconds and stops caring. Cheapest alternative: set a single hardcoded faster tick rate — one-line change, 0 logic, gains initial challenge but loses the learning curve for beginners. This proposal buys a structured challenge arc; it does NOT buy adaptive difficulty based on reaction time, enemy AI, boss levels, or unlockable skins.

## Out of scope

_none_

## Blockers (external / decisions needed)

_none_

---
*Imported from Arden proposal `snake-003`.*

title: In-page restart without reload (R key + death-screen button)
description:
## Summary

On game-over, display a death screen overlay on the canvas with the final score and a 'Play Again' button. Pressing R or clicking the button resets game state in memory and restarts the loop — no page reload required. Score resets; high-score is preserved in memory for the session.

## Code references



## Detail

Benefits the replay-loop player grinding for a high score who loses flow every time F5 reloads the full page and clears the score display. Cheapest alternative: wire F5 reload to a button — zero JS logic but wipes session high-score and requires page parse. This proposal buys instant in-memory restart; it does NOT buy persistent leaderboard storage, undo-last-move, or continuation from death position.

## Out of scope

_none_

## Blockers (external / decisions needed)

_none_

---
*Imported from Arden proposal `snake-002`.*

title: Pause/resume overlay (P key)
description:
## Summary

Add P-key toggle that freezes the requestAnimationFrame loop and renders a semi-transparent 'PAUSED' label on the canvas. A second P keypress resumes from the exact game state. Escape also pauses.

## Code references



## Detail

Benefits the casual lunchtime player who gets interrupted mid-run and loses their best score to a forced restart. Cheapest alternative: do nothing — the player can reload the page, which costs zero dev-hours but zero UX satisfaction. This proposal buys deliberate mid-game suspension; it does NOT buy save-state persistence across page close, a pause menu with settings, or an AFK auto-pause timer.

## Out of scope

_none_

## Blockers (external / decisions needed)

_none_

---
*Imported from Arden proposal `snake-001`.*

title: Minimal Vitest unit tests for core game logic
description:
## Summary

Add vitest as a dev dependency. Extract three pure functions from game.js into a testable module (moveSnake, checkCollision, checkFoodCollision) with no DOM dependencies. Write tests for: normal movement, wall collision in wall-death mode, wrap-around boundary in wrap mode, food eaten increments score and grows snake. Target: <10 test cases, <100 lines. Add 'test' npm script.

## Code references



## Detail

Benefits the developer who refactors the collision detection logic (e.g., to support wrap mode from snake-004) and breaks wall-death silently — a class of bug invisible to casual play until edge conditions are hit. Cheapest alternative: manual play-test after each change — free, catches obvious regressions, but misses boundary conditions like the snake being exactly 1 cell from the wall in wrap mode. This proposal buys regression coverage for the three most-changed functions; it does NOT buy canvas rendering tests, E2E browser automation, CI integration, or mutation testing.

## Out of scope

_none_

## Blockers (external / decisions needed)

_none_

---
*Imported from Arden proposal `snake-012`.*

title: ESLint flat config + npm run lint
description:
## Summary

Add eslint.config.js (flat config, eslint 9+) with recommended JS rules and a no-console warning. Add package.json with a lint script: 'eslint game.js'. Add .eslintignore or globs to skip test fixtures. Document the lint step in README. No CI wiring in this proposal.

## Code references



## Detail

Benefits the first-time contributor who introduces a var hoisting bug or undefined variable and only discovers it at runtime. Cheapest alternative: Biome (biome.sh) — faster, zero-config, single binary, catches the same class of errors. Chosen ESLint here because the dark-factory game targets JS learners more familiar with the ESLint ecosystem and its error messages. This proposal buys local lint on save; it does NOT buy a pre-commit hook, CI enforcement, type checking via tsc, or automated fix-on-save configuration.

## Out of scope

_none_

## Blockers (external / decisions needed)

_none_

---
*Imported from Arden proposal `snake-011`.*

title: Extract inline JS to game.js
description:
## Summary

Move all <script> block content from index.html into a sibling game.js. Add type='module' to the <script src='game.js'> tag in index.html so the script gets module scope by default. No logic changes. Update any implicit globals to explicit module-level exports where needed by tests.

## Code references



## Detail

Benefits the developer adding a feature (e.g., a leaderboard) who wants editor intellisense, go-to-definition, and ESLint running on the game script rather than parsing it as an HTML island. Cheapest alternative: add <script type='module'> inline — gains module scope and deferred execution with zero new files. This proposal additionally gives the file its own editor buffer and enables import/export for the test setup in snake-012; it does NOT buy a bundler, tree shaking, dynamic import, or hot-module replacement.

## Out of scope

_none_

## Blockers (external / decisions needed)

_none_

---
*Imported from Arden proposal `snake-010`.*

title: Extract inline CSS to style.css
description:
## Summary

Move all <style> block content from index.html into a sibling style.css. Update index.html with a single <link rel='stylesheet' href='style.css'>. No functional changes. Normalize whitespace and ordering (reset → layout → components → utilities).

## Code references



## Detail

Benefits the developer who wants to edit the dark-factory theme or tune colors without parsing 300+ lines of mixed HTML. Cheapest alternative: add a structured CSS comment block inside the inline <style> tag — zero file-system change, gains navigation, but editors still treat it as HTML context and lint/format tools skip it. This proposal buys proper CSS editor tooling (lint, format, intellisense); it does NOT buy CSS custom properties, design tokens, CSS modules, or scoped styles.

## Out of scope

_none_

## Blockers (external / decisions needed)

_none_

---
*Imported from Arden proposal `snake-009`.*
